name: Andouille in Comforting Barbecue Sauce
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Louisiana hot sauce OR 2 tablespoons Cayenne pepper
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat the peanut oil in a large pot over medium heat.
2. Add onions and cook until clear or tender, about 5 minutes.
3. Add celery and continue cooking for another 5 minutes.
4. Stir in bell pepper and parsley; cook for an additional 5 minutes.
5. Add garlic and stir for 1 more minute.
6. Pour in steak sauce and hot sauce (or cayenne pepper), stirring well.
7. Season with salt to taste.
8. Mix in the Southern Comfort Liquor.
9. Bring mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer uncovered for 2-3 hours until thickened.
10. Combine 1 cup of the sauce with sliced 1 pound Andouille sausage (sliced 1/4 inch thick).
11. Cook the sausage well on the stove or in a chafing dish until heated through.
12. Serve hot with French bread or teethpicks for easy eating; use napkins as needed.
"Other smoked sausages may be used, but we like andouille."
